points.text: Monte Rosa Therapeutics (Nasdaq: GLUE) is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing a pipeline of first-in-class and only-in-class molecular glue degrader (MGD) medicines for autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology indications.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics utilizes its QuEEN™ discovery engine combining AI-guided chemistry and proteomics to design MGDs with unprecedented selectivity.
SEED Therapeutics is advancing ST-01156, an orally administered, selective RBM39 molecular glue degrader, in a first-in-human Phase 1 study for advanced solid malignancies.
SEED Therapeutics employs its proprietary RITE3™ platform to develop six molecular glue programs across oncology, neurodegeneration, and immunology.
SEED Therapeutics — ST-01156 — Advanced solid malignancies (RBM39-dependent) — First-in-human Phase 1 study presented at ASCO 2026 to characterize safety, pharmacology, and early activity signals.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular Glue Degrader Pipeline — Autoimmune, oncology, neurology — Three programs currently in the clinic with ongoing collaborations with leading pharmaceutical companies.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Clinical-stage MGD development — QuEEN™ platform and three clinical programs — Participating in TD Cowen Oncology Innovation Summit (May 27, 2026) and Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference (June 4, 2026).

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Molecular glue degrader (MGD) commercialization — QuEEN™ platform + 3 clinical programs — Leading pipeline of first-in-class MGDs; ongoing collaborations with major pharma in immunology, oncology, and neurology.

SEED Therapeutics — Rationally designed molecular glue degraders — ST-01156 (RBM39 degrader) and six total programs — Co-founded by Nobel Laureate Avram Hershko; presenting Phase 1 trial design at ASCO 2026.

ST-01156 — Mechanism: Selective RBM39 molecular glue degrader — Phase: 1 (First-in-Human) — Population: Advanced solid malignancies, RBM39-dependent cancers — Design: Oral, brain-penetrant; trial structured to assess safety, pharmacology, and early activity.

Monte Rosa MGDs — Mechanism: Small molecule protein degraders targeting neosubstrates via QuEEN™ engine — Phase: Clinical (3 programs) — Population: Patients with serious autoimmune, inflammatory, and oncology diseases — Differentiation: Unprecedented selectivity vs other degrader modalities.
Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Investor Relations — Management participation in TD Cowen 7th Annual Oncology Innovation Summit (May 27, 2026) and Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference (June 4, 2026).
SEED Therapeutics — Scientific Dissemination — Presentation of ST-01156 Phase 1 trial design at ASCO 2026 (May 29–June 2, Chicago) under abstract TPS3164.
